meta_title: Tax Questions Answered by AI | SupaTax AI
meta_description_hint: Browse 150+ free AI-powered answers to the most common US tax questions.

meta_title: What Is Backup Withholding? (IRS Rules Explained)
meta_description_hint: Backup withholding is a 24% federal tax the IRS requires payers to withhold when you haven't provided a valid TIN. Here's exactly when it applies.

meta_title: Is Homeowners Insurance Tax Deductible? (2025 IRS Rules)
meta_description_hint: Homeowners insurance is NOT deductible for your personal home. But rental property owners can deduct it fully. We explain every exception.

meta_title: What Is Medicare Tax? Rates, Exemptions & Who Pays (2025)
meta_description_hint: Medicare tax is a 1.45% payroll tax on all wages, with an additional 0.9% for high earners. Self-employed pay 2.9% total. Full breakdown here.

meta_title: Are Funeral Expenses Tax Deductible? (Personal vs Estate)
meta_description_hint: Funeral expenses are NOT deductible on your personal return but ARE deductible on an estate tax return (Form 706). Full IRS rules here.

meta_title: Are Union Dues Tax Deductible in 2025? (TCJA Impact)
meta_description_hint: Union dues are no longer deductible on federal returns under the 2017 TCJA through 2025. Some states still allow the deduction. Full state-by-state guide.

meta_title: What Is SDI Tax? (State Disability Insurance Explained)
meta_description_hint: SDI tax is a California payroll deduction of 1.1% (2025 rate) that funds the state's disability and paid family leave program. Here's who pays it.

meta_title: SDI Tax: What It Is and How It Works in California (2025)
meta_description_hint: SDI (State Disability Insurance) is a California payroll tax at 1.1% of wages in 2025. We cover the rate, who pays it, and how to claim benefits.

meta_title: Are GoFundMe Donations Tax Deductible? (IRS Rules 2025)
meta_description_hint: GoFundMe donations to personal campaigns are NOT tax deductible. Donations through GoFundMe Charity may be deductible if the recipient is a 501(c)(3).

meta_title: Is Car Insurance Tax Deductible? (Business vs Personal Use)
meta_description_hint: Car insurance is NOT deductible for personal vehicles. If you use your car for business, you can deduct the business-use portion. Full IRS rules inside.

meta_title: Are Closing Costs Tax Deductible? (What You Can and Can't Claim)
meta_description_hint: Most closing costs are NOT immediately deductible. Mortgage points and property taxes paid at closing may be deductible. Full breakdown by line item.

target_keyword: if i pay child support can i claim my child on taxes
meta_title: If I Pay Child Support Can I Claim My Child on Taxes? (2025 IRS Rules)
meta_description_hint: Paying child support does NOT automatically give you the right to claim the child dependent exemption. That right belongs to the custodial parent by default.

target_keyword: why is receiving a large tax refund a bad thing
meta_title: Why Is Receiving a Large Tax Refund a Bad Thing? (Financial Experts Explain)
meta_description_hint: A large tax refund means you overpaid the IRS all year — giving them an interest-free loan of your own money. Here's what to do instead.

target_keyword: what is tax exempt interest income
meta_title: What Is Tax-Exempt Interest Income? (Form 1040 Line 2a)
meta_description_hint: Tax-exempt interest income is interest from municipal bonds or other federally exempt sources. It goes on Form 1040 Line 2a and is not taxed federally.

meta_title: Inheritance Tax in California: Does It Exist? (2025 Guide)
meta_description_hint: California has NO inheritance tax and NO estate tax. Inherited assets may have capital gains implications, but there's no California-specific death tax.

meta_title: Do Gift Cards Have Tax? (Sales Tax, Income Tax & Gift Tax Rules)
meta_description_hint: Gift card taxation depends on the context: employee gift cards are taxable income. Customer gift cards trigger tax when redeemed, not purchased. Full rules.

meta_title: Backup Withholding: What It Is and How to Stop It (2025)
meta_description_hint: Backup withholding is a 24% flat rate the IRS forces payers to withhold when your TIN is missing or incorrect. Here's what triggers it and how to fix it.

meta_title: IRS TREAS 310 TAX REF: What This Deposit Means (2025)
meta_description_hint: IRS TREAS 310 TAX REF is a direct deposit code for federal tax refunds. If you see it in your bank account, the IRS has processed and sent your refund.

target_keyword: how long does it take for tax refund to show in bank account after approved
meta_title: How Long for Tax Refund to Show in Bank After Approved? (2025 IRS Timeline)
meta_description_hint: After approval, your refund usually appears in your bank account within 1–5 business days. E-filed returns: 21 days total. Paper returns: 6–8 weeks. Full timeline.

meta_title: Tax-Exempt Interest Income: What Counts and How to Report It
meta_description_hint: Tax-exempt interest income includes municipal bond interest and US territory bond interest. It's reported on Form 1040 Line 2a but not included in taxable income.

meta_title: California Capital Gains Tax Rate 2025: What You'll Pay
meta_description_hint: California taxes capital gains as ordinary income — rates from 1% to 13.3%. No special long-term rate. One of the highest capital gains taxes in the US.

meta_title: AFR Rates (Applicable Federal Rates) — March 2026 Update
meta_description_hint: Applicable Federal Rates (AFR) are IRS-set minimum interest rates for family loans and deferred payments. Published monthly. Current rates listed here.

meta_title: Are Medicare Premiums Tax Deductible in 2025?
meta_description_hint: Medicare Part B and D premiums are deductible as medical expenses if you itemize AND they exceed 7.5% of AGI. Self-employed people can deduct 100%.

meta_title: Are 401k Contributions Tax Deductible? (2025 Limits)
meta_description_hint: Traditional 401k contributions reduce your taxable income dollar-for-dollar. 2025 limit is $23,500 ($31,000 if 50+). Roth 401k contributions are NOT deductible.

target_keyword: do you report roth ira on taxes
meta_title: Do You Report Roth IRA on Taxes? (What to File and What to Skip)
meta_description_hint: Roth IRA contributions are NOT reported on your tax return. Roth IRA earnings are also tax-free in retirement. The only reporting needed: early withdrawal on Form 5329.

meta_title: Is Severance Pay Taxable? (Federal + State Rules 2025)
meta_description_hint: Yes, severance pay is fully taxable as ordinary income. Federal income tax, Social Security, and Medicare taxes all apply. Your employer withholds at the supplemental rate.

meta_title: Qualifying Surviving Spouse (Widow/Widower): Tax Status Explained
meta_description_hint: Qualifying Surviving Spouse (formerly Qualifying Widower) lets you use Married Filing Jointly rates for 2 years after spouse's death if you have a dependent child.

meta_title: Does California Tax Social Security Benefits? (2025)
meta_description_hint: California does NOT tax Social Security benefits — one of the few states that fully exempts them at the state level. Federal taxes may still apply.

meta_title: Capital Gains Tax in Florida: Does the State Tax It? (2025)
meta_description_hint: Florida has NO state income tax and NO capital gains tax. You only owe federal capital gains tax (0%, 15%, or 20% depending on income). Full breakdown.

meta_title: Form 1099-C: Cancellation of Debt — Do You Owe Taxes?
meta_description_hint: A 1099-C means a creditor cancelled $600+ of debt. The cancelled amount is generally taxable income UNLESS you qualify for an insolvency or bankruptcy exclusion.

meta_title: IRS TREAS 310: What It Means When You See It in Your Bank
meta_description_hint: IRS TREAS 310 is the standard ACH deposit code for IRS payments including tax refunds, stimulus payments, and other government disbursements. See full list.

target_keyword: if you pay child support can you claim the child on your taxes 2026
meta_title: If You Pay Child Support Can You Claim the Child on Taxes? (2026 Rules)
meta_description_hint: Paying child support does NOT entitle you to claim the child. The custodial parent has that right by default. Only a Form 8332 release from custodial parent changes this.

target_keyword: what is tax liability zero mean
meta_title: What Does Zero Tax Liability Mean? (And How to Achieve It Legally)
meta_description_hint: Zero tax liability means you owe no federal income tax after applying all credits and deductions. You can still owe FICA taxes. Here's how some people achieve it.

meta_title: California Inheritance Tax: Why There Isn't One (and What Applies Instead)
meta_description_hint: California repealed its inheritance tax in 1982. There is no state inheritance or estate tax. Federal estate tax may apply to estates over $13.61M (2025).

meta_title: Prop 19 Loopholes in California: What Still Works in 2025
meta_description_hint: Proposition 19 eliminated most parent-to-child property tax exclusions in 2021. But there are still strategies: primary residence transfers and legal entity restructuring.

target_keyword: is social security taxable in california
meta_title: Is Social Security Taxable in California? (State + Federal Rules)
meta_description_hint: Social Security is NOT taxable in California. However, up to 85% of your benefits may be federally taxable depending on your combined income. Full breakdown.

meta_title: Taxable Interest Income: What It Is and How to Report It (1040 Line 2b)
meta_description_hint: Taxable interest includes bank account interest, CD interest, and most bond interest. It's reported on Form 1040 Line 2b and taxed as ordinary income.

meta_title: Is Mortgage Insurance Tax Deductible in 2025?
meta_description_hint: The mortgage insurance premium deduction expired after 2021 under federal law. It is NOT currently available for 2025 unless Congress extends it. State rules vary.

target_keyword: can father claim child on taxes if child does not live with him
meta_title: Can a Father Claim a Child on Taxes If the Child Doesn't Live With Him?
meta_description_hint: A non-custodial father can claim the child ONLY if the custodial parent signs Form 8332 releasing the dependent exemption. Paying child support alone is not enough.

meta_title: Unreimbursed Employee Expenses: Can You Deduct Them in 2025?
meta_description_hint: Unreimbursed employee expenses (W-2 workers) are NOT deductible federally through 2025 due to TCJA. Some states (CA, NY) still allow this deduction.

meta_title: Are Disability Benefits Taxable? (SSDI vs SSI vs Private)
meta_description_hint: SSDI may be taxable (up to 85%) if your total income exceeds IRS thresholds. SSI is never taxable. Private disability benefits taxability depends on who paid the premiums.

target_keyword: is private school tuition tax deductible
meta_title: Is Private School Tuition Tax Deductible? (Federal vs State 2025)
meta_description_hint: Private school tuition is NOT federally tax deductible. However, 529 plan funds can be used for K-12 private school tuition up to $10,000/year. State credits vary.

meta_title: Backup Withholding Meaning: The Simple Definition and When It Applies
meta_description_hint: Backup withholding means the IRS requires a 24% tax holdback on your payments because your taxpayer ID is missing, incorrect, or flagged for under-reporting.

meta_title: Is Home Insurance Tax Deductible? (2025 Answer)
meta_description_hint: Home insurance (homeowners insurance) is NOT tax deductible for your personal residence. Rental property home insurance IS deductible on Schedule E.

meta_title: Do LLCs Get 1099 Forms? (Single Member vs Multi-Member Rules)
meta_description_hint: Single-member LLCs taxed as sole props DO get 1099-NEC forms. S-Corp and C-Corp LLCs generally do NOT. Partnership LLCs DO get 1099s. Full breakdown.

meta_title: What Is the Zero Tax Program? (Legal Tax Elimination Strategies)
meta_description_hint: The 'Zero Tax Program' refers to IRS-legal strategies for eliminating federal income tax liability through deductions, credits, and retirement contributions. Here's what's real.

meta_title: Job Expenses for W-2 Employees: What You Can (and Can't) Deduct
meta_description_hint: W-2 employees cannot deduct job expenses federally through 2025. Exceptions: educator expenses ($300), home office for remote workers varies by state. Full guide.

target_keyword: can you file head of household if married
meta_title: Can You File Head of Household If Married? (IRS Rules)
meta_description_hint: Married people generally cannot file Head of Household UNLESS they meet the 'considered unmarried' test: lived apart from spouse the last 6 months of the year.

meta_title: Tax Abatement Meaning: What It Is and How to Apply
meta_description_hint: A tax abatement is a government reduction or elimination of taxes for a specific period — commonly used for real estate development incentives or first-time penalty relief.

meta_description_hint: Everything about IRS TREAS 310 bank deposits — what each code means, how long to wait, what to do if you didn't expect it.

meta_description_hint: Everything about backup withholding — what triggers it, the 24% rate, how to stop it, and what forms to file.

meta_description_hint: Every tax deduction available to homeowners — mortgage interest, property tax, home office, closing costs, and more.

meta_description_hint: Complete breakdown of Medicare tax (1.45%/2.9%), Social Security tax (6.2%/12.4%), IRMAA surcharges, and state exemptions.

meta_description_hint: Comprehensive guide to California state taxes — capital gains rates, Social Security exemption, SDI, inheritance tax, and Prop 19.
